codeReview.summary: The feature is implemented as scoped test/evidence work and it now directly proves the required sensitive-contained enum, nested-override coexistence, and mixed batch isolation contracts without changing production runtime behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still leaves Windows init handling ambiguous. This worker attempted to execute .factory/init.sh directly via PowerShell even though mission/global guidance says .sh files must be read as text and translated to native commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session 318fe76a-299a-4835-88bb-17c8b5544c82 records an Execute step invoking .factory/init.sh, while handoff.skillFeedback.deviations explicitly says Windows rejected direct .sh execution and suggests updating the skill guidance.

summary: Reviewed commit 376c1a87 and the worker transcript/handoff. The added integration regressions explicitly cover VAL-ER-002, VAL-CROSS-003, and VAL-CROSS-005, and the compile-fail stderr refresh matches the approved SensitiveValueOf<T> secure-container seam. No blocking or non-blocking code issues were found.
